goal: SDK intake to Core-Node parallel fanout with unified comparison receipt
assumptions: SDK remains the user-facing intake boundary.
Core-Node owns execution-path selection and parallel fanout comparison.
master-records remains an independent witness loop and is not part of the user return loop.
All paths must return the same human-readable result shape with compute cost included.

fanout_paths.name: return-path memory micro-node path
fanout_paths.role: known return path with memory retention comparison path

fanout_paths.name: adaptive cell assembly path
fanout_paths.role: measured incoming task assembles transition cells on demand

activation_criteria: SDK produces a fanout request artifact for the same sample package.
Core-Node fanout request references PATH-1 through PATH-5.
Unified comparison receipt includes result status, path id, elapsed time, memory, estimated cost, receipt count, and warning state for every path.
Human-readable result uses the same shape for every path.
Witness loop is represented as independent hashes or receipt references, not as part of the user return loop.
CI validates the goal document and sample fanout request artifacts.
